query_normalized: predicting glass transition temperature from compositional descriptors with explainable boosting machines materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (f) as a foundational methodology and canonical reference for the "Explainable Boosting Machines" and "explainable machine learning" components of the user's question within the materials science domain. While it is a review of techniques rather than a specific study on glass transition temperature, it provides the essential methodological framework and context that a literature review on this topic would require to justify the choice of explainable models.
